params.name: ids
params.description: The requested list of security identifiers. Accepted ID types include Market Tickers, SEDOL, ISINs, CUSIPs, or FactSet Permanent Ids. Limit of 1,000 per non-batch request / 2,000 per batch request (GET) or 30,000 per batch request (POST).

params.name: metrics
params.description: Requested list of Financial Statement Items or Ratios. Use the `/metrics` endpoint for a complete list of available FF_* metric items. When requesting multiple metrics, you cannot mix metric data types (e.g. strings and floats). Limit of 1,600 per request.

params.name: periodicity
params.description: Periodicity or frequency of the fiscal periods. ANN = Annual Original, ANN_R = Annual Latest (includes restatements), QTR = Quarterly Original, QTR_R = Quarterly Latest, SEMI = Semi-Annual, SEMI_R = Semi-Annual Latest, LTM = Last Twelve Months, LTM_R = Last Twelve Months Latest, LTMSG = Last Twelve Months Global, LTM_SEMI = Last Twelve Months Semi-Annual, YTD = Year-to-date.

params.name: fiscalPeriodStart
params.description: The fiscal period start expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recent completed period during resolution.

params.name: fiscalPeriodEnd
params.description: The fiscal period end expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recently completed period during resolution.

params.name: currency
params.description: Currency code for currency values. For a list of currency ISO codes, visit Online Assistant Page 1470. Use "DOC" for reporting currency. Defaults to LOCAL.

params.name: updateType
params.description: Update status flag. RP = Include preliminary data, RF = Only final data.

params.name: batch
params.description: Enables asynchronous batch processing, supporting long-running requests up to 20 minutes. Upon requesting batch=Y, the service will respond with HTTP 202. Use batch-status to check completion and batch-result to retrieve results.

items.docs:
Retrieves FactSet Fundamental standardized data for specified securities. Use the `/metrics` endpoint to retrieve a full list of valid metrics or data items.

The `/fundamentals` endpoint currently supports Long Running asynchronous requests up to **20 minutes** via batch parameter. Id limits are increased to 2000 ids per request when using batch capability. This 2000 id limit has been derived based on single metric for one day.

info.name: Factset Returns Company Fundamental Data for a List of Identifiers.

items.docs:
Retrieves FactSet Fundamental standardized data for specified securities using a POST request body. Use the `/metrics` endpoint to retrieve a full list of valid metrics or data items.

The `/fundamentals` endpoint currently supports Long Running asynchronous requests up to **20 minutes** via batch parameter. Id limits are increased to 30,000 ids per request when using batch capability. This 30,000 id limit has been derived based on single metric for one day.

info.name: Factset Returns Financial Statement Line Items.

params.name: ids
params.description: The requested list of security identifiers. Accepted ID types include Market Tickers, SEDOL, ISINs, CUSIPs, or FactSet Permanent Ids. Limit of 1,000 per non-batch request / 2,000 per batch request (GET) or 30,000 per batch request (POST).

params.name: metrics
params.description: Requested list of Financial Statement Items or Ratios. Use the `/metrics` endpoint for a complete list of available FF_* metric items. When requesting multiple metrics, you cannot mix metric data types (e.g. strings and floats). Limit of 1,600 per request.

params.name: category
params.description: Filter financial statements by type. INCOME_STATEMENT for revenue and earnings data, BALANCE_SHEET for assets and liabilities, CASH_FLOW for cash flow activities, FINANCIAL_SERVICES for financial institution specific items.

params.name: periodicity
params.description: Periodicity or frequency of the fiscal periods. ANN = Annual Original, ANN_R = Annual Latest (includes restatements), QTR = Quarterly Original, QTR_R = Quarterly Latest, SEMI = Semi-Annual, SEMI_R = Semi-Annual Latest, LTM = Last Twelve Months, LTM_R = Last Twelve Months Latest, LTMSG = Last Twelve Months Global, LTM_SEMI = Last Twelve Months Semi-Annual, YTD = Year-to-date.

params.name: fiscalPeriodStart
params.description: The fiscal period start expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recent completed period during resolution.

params.name: fiscalPeriodEnd
params.description: The fiscal period end expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recently completed period during resolution.

params.name: currency
params.description: Currency code for currency values. For a list of currency ISO codes, visit Online Assistant Page 1470. Use "DOC" for reporting currency. Defaults to LOCAL.

params.name: updateType
params.description: Update status flag. RP = Include preliminary data, RF = Only final data.

items.docs:
Retrieves detailed financial statement data organized by statement type (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow). Use the `category` parameter to filter by statement type, and the `metrics` parameter to request specific line items.

Available statement categories include INCOME_STATEMENT, BALANCE_SHEET, CASH_FLOW, and FINANCIAL_SERVICES for financial institutions.

info.name: Factset Returns Financial Statement Line Items for a List of Identifiers.

items.docs: Retrieves detailed financial statement data via POST for large identifier sets. Supports up to 30,000 ids per batch request.

params.name: ids
params.description: The requested list of security identifiers. Accepted ID types include Market Tickers, SEDOL, ISINs, CUSIPs, or FactSet Permanent Ids. Limit of 1,000 per non-batch request / 2,000 per batch request (GET) or 30,000 per batch request (POST).

params.name: metrics
params.description: Requested list of ratio metrics. Common ratios include FF_PE (price-to-earnings), FF_PBK (price-to-book), FF_ROE (return on equity), FF_DEBT_EQUITY, FF_CURR_RATIO, and others. Use the `/metrics` endpoint with category=RATIOS for a complete list.

params.name: subcategory
params.description: Filter ratios by subcategory. FINANCIAL for leverage and coverage ratios, GROWTH_RATE for revenue and earnings growth, LIQUIDITY for current and quick ratios, PROFITABILITY for margin and return metrics, SIZE for market cap and enterprise value, VALUATION for PE, PB, and dividend yield.

params.name: periodicity
params.description: Periodicity or frequency of the fiscal periods. ANN = Annual Original, ANN_R = Annual Latest (includes restatements), QTR = Quarterly Original, QTR_R = Quarterly Latest, SEMI = Semi-Annual, SEMI_R = Semi-Annual Latest, LTM = Last Twelve Months, LTM_R = Last Twelve Months Latest, LTMSG = Last Twelve Months Global, LTM_SEMI = Last Twelve Months Semi-Annual, YTD = Year-to-date.

params.name: fiscalPeriodStart
params.description: The fiscal period start expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recent completed period during resolution.

params.name: fiscalPeriodEnd
params.description: The fiscal period end expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recently completed period during resolution.

params.name: currency
params.description: Currency code for currency values. For a list of currency ISO codes, visit Online Assistant Page 1470. Use "DOC" for reporting currency. Defaults to LOCAL.

items.docs:
Retrieves pre-calculated financial ratios for specified securities. Available ratio subcategories include FINANCIAL, GROWTH_RATE, LIQUIDITY, PROFITABILITY, SIZE, and VALUATION.

Common ratios include price-to-earnings (FF_PE), return on equity (FF_ROE), debt-to-equity (FF_DEBT_EQUITY), current ratio (FF_CURR_RATIO), and many others.

info.name: Factset Returns Pre-calculated Financial Ratios for a List of Identifiers.

items.docs: Retrieves pre-calculated financial ratios via POST for large identifier sets.

params.name: ids
params.description: The requested list of security identifiers. Accepted ID types include Market Tickers, SEDOL, ISINs, CUSIPs, or FactSet Permanent Ids. Limit of 1,000 per non-batch request / 2,000 per batch request (GET) or 30,000 per batch request (POST).

params.name: metrics
params.description: Requested list of estimate metrics. Common estimate items include FF_EST_SALES, FF_EST_EPS, FF_EST_EBITDA, FF_EST_NET_INC. Use the `/metrics` endpoint for a complete list of available estimate items.

params.name: periodicity
params.description: Periodicity or frequency of the fiscal periods. ANN = Annual Original, ANN_R = Annual Latest (includes restatements), QTR = Quarterly Original, QTR_R = Quarterly Latest, SEMI = Semi-Annual, SEMI_R = Semi-Annual Latest, LTM = Last Twelve Months, LTM_R = Last Twelve Months Latest, LTMSG = Last Twelve Months Global, LTM_SEMI = Last Twelve Months Semi-Annual, YTD = Year-to-date.

params.name: fiscalPeriodStart
params.description: The fiscal period start expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recent completed period during resolution.

params.name: fiscalPeriodEnd
params.description: The fiscal period end expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recently completed period during resolution.

params.name: currency
params.description: Currency code for currency values. For a list of currency ISO codes, visit Online Assistant Page 1470. Use "DOC" for reporting currency. Defaults to LOCAL.

items.docs:
Retrieves consensus analyst estimates for specified securities and metrics. Consensus estimates are aggregated from a wide base of over 800+ contributors and cover over 19,000 active companies across 90+ countries.

Common estimate metrics include FF_EST_SALES (estimated sales), FF_EST_EPS (estimated earnings per share), and FF_EST_EBITDA (estimated EBITDA).

info.name: Factset Returns Consensus Analyst Estimates for a List of Identifiers.

items.docs: Retrieves consensus analyst estimates via POST for large identifier sets. Supports up to 30,000 ids per batch request.

params.name: ids
params.description: The requested list of security identifiers. Accepted ID types include Market Tickers, SEDOL, ISINs, CUSIPs, or FactSet Permanent Ids. Limit of 1,000 per non-batch request / 2,000 per batch request (GET) or 30,000 per batch request (POST).

params.name: metrics
params.description: Segment data metric. SALES = Sales/Revenue, OPINC = Operating Income/Loss, ASSETS = Total Assets, DEP = Depreciation Expense, CAPEX = Capital Expenditures.

params.name: segmentType
params.description: Segment type for the metrics. BUS = Business segments, GEO = Geographic segments.

params.name: periodicity
params.description: Periodicity for segment data. ANN = Annual Original, ANN_R = Annual Latest (includes restatements).

params.name: fiscalPeriodStart
params.description: The fiscal period start expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recent completed period during resolution.

params.name: fiscalPeriodEnd
params.description: The fiscal period end expressed as YYYY-MM-DD. Calendar date that will fall back to the most recently completed period during resolution.

params.name: batch
params.description: Enables asynchronous batch processing, supporting long-running requests up to 20 minutes. Upon requesting batch=Y, the service will respond with HTTP 202. Use batch-status to check completion and batch-result to retrieve results.

items.docs:
Retrieves business and geographic segment data for individual companies. Segment metrics include SALES, OPINC (operating income), ASSETS, DEP (depreciation), and CAPEX (capital expenditures).

The `/segments` endpoint supports Long Running asynchronous requests up to **20 minutes** via batch parameter. Id limits are increased to 2,000 ids per request when using batch capability.

info.name: Factset Returns Company Segment Data for a List of Identifiers.

items.docs: Retrieves segment data via POST for large identifier sets. Supports up to 30,000 ids per batch request.

info.name: Returns Available Factset Fundamental Metrics and Ratios.

params.name: category
params.description: Filters the list of FF_* metrics by major category. Categories include INCOME_STATEMENT, BALANCE_SHEET, CASH_FLOW, RATIOS, FINANCIAL_SERVICES, INDUSTRY_METRICS, PENSION_AND_POSTRETIREMENT, MARKET_DATA, MISCELLANEOUS, and DATES.

params.name: subcategory
params.description: Sub-category filter for the primary category requested. Choose a related sub-category for the category requested. For example, RATIOS supports FINANCIAL, GROWTH_RATE, LIQUIDITY, PROFITABILITY, SIZE, and VALUATION.

params.name: metricDataType
params.description: Filter metrics by data type. Returns only metrics matching the specified type (integer, float, or string).

items.docs:
Returns list of available FF_* metrics that can be used in the `metrics` parameter of related endpoints. These are related to FactSet Fundamentals standardized data. Leave Category and Subcategory blank to request all available items.

For methodology definitions, reference the `oaPageId` or `oaUrl` response items to launch the available methodology page.

info.name: Factset Returns the Status for a Batch Request.

params.name: id
params.description: Batch request identifier (UUID).

items.docs: Return the status for the underlying batch request that is specified by the id.

info.name: Factset Returns the Response for a Batch Request.

params.name: id
params.description: Batch request identifier (UUID).

items.docs:
Returns the response data for the underlying batch request that is specified by the id.

By default, this endpoint will return data as JSON. If you wish to receive your data in CSV format, you can edit the header to have the "accept" parameter as "text/csv" instead of "application/json".
